evidence.text: The company really tries to provide good benefits and good pay to their employees. As of August 2025, the system-wide minimum wage for *any* position is $19 / hr. Benefits are very good, not the best, but very good and it goes beyond the health insurance and retirement - employees get 24/7 access to fitness centers, there are numerous programs to aid in your overall wellness (mentally / emotionally and physically) a...

evidence.role: Current employee - senior lead pharmacy technician
evidence.text: Great benefits like 10 free mental health counseling services. Pay is above average and there are merit raises and market adjustments. Great culture and CEO involvement. Decent pay, good benefits
Good pay, benefits, compensation, healthcare Good pay
Good pay, great benefits. Small bonus throughout the year, excellent chance to grow in company. Great organization to grow with.

The only reason I gave 4 stars instead of 5 is because the health benefits are pricey and require you to use Houston Methodist, which is well known to be the most expensive hospital system in the greater Houston area. So my healthcare expenses increased significantly when I started working here. But the culture is excellent and the other benefits are phenomenal. So I plan to stay with them until I retire. Just wish...
Friendly environment, fast growth and opportunity to learn Desktop support technician

Patients and clinical mission are meaningful Many frontline coworkers are hardworking and supportive High patient volume means you learn quickly Some units have strong peer-to-peer teamwork Strong brand name, weak employee support and accountability -Horrible Place to work
Good environment to work in. Positive work environment and good teamwork.

evidence.role: Former employee - Respiratory Therapist New Grad
evidence.text: Friendly environment, strong teamwork. Yea! Ok

Company wants you to feel valued They set high standards About the Culture
Supportive management and leadership Opportunities to gain new skills and education Great place to work
A company who preached about: - Integrity - Caring - Accountability - Respect - Excellent A good compensation and decent benefit No career growth in IT division if you were not in management ranks
Great support for career growth and job search. They encourage growth and even help pay for college. Great Place!
Makes it clear that employees are valued and develops for promotion from within. Great Employer
Great work environment, good work life balance Work Life Balance

Have to approved by administration for extra hours. Assignments large for 1 PCA with most units only having 2-3 PCAs at best. Understaffed, and low pay

evidence.role: Former employee - Virtual Patient Observation Technician
evidence.text: Low pay. They pay more to new hires. Low pay

Manager is racist, descriminatory to ethnic minority ( she has trauma of childhood abuse), takes collegues help to harass picked ones. Has color preference. Directors are involved too. Harassment increased when racial abuse was reported to director, senior director and HR. They are thugs in tuxedo. The way one of the directors talk felt like mafia don ( mexican cartel ). I CARE value is only lip service, its I Consi...
Supply Chain is very toxic work environment. No processes in place Supply Chain is Toxic

Houston Methodist has a very toxic environment that is rampant with bullying and racism. The turn over rate there is extremely high from management on down. It is extremely hard to move through out the hospital duevto being blocked by management to retain staff on their more unfavorable floors. Management constantly turns a blind eye to abusive treatment amongst staff members and some times even encourage it. The st...
If you are not Hispanic, do not go into EVS. There is a lot of racism and politics. HR, supervisors and directors are aware but nothing is being done. Meh
Compensation is low Nepotism and favoritism runs rampant Location Management Just Fine

Management does not support career growth Leadership lacks attention towards peers when it comes to career goals Leadership does not want to advance in your career Current manager is micro manager and talks to employees in degrading manner Management and Leadership is Not Supportive

- poor management and no leadership direction. No accountability from the leadership. If they can pass the bucket to you, they will. They are not in line with what they preach such as the ICARE values. No innovation is encouraged or allowed. Although, the company provides an annual training in which they encourage innovation, unparalleled service, etc. - if you work in IT, this is a dead-end job place. No real in-cl...
Aggressive and indirect discrimination. Unrealistic expectations of Staff RN. Sink or Swim mentality with micromanagement, Civil incivility. (2019-2022). Regular Pay
There are not many downsides Great Company to Work For
There isn't much opportunity for advancement. Unsupportive directors. Some Changes Are Needed
Career advancement: They only hire from contractors nepo babies certain colleges like texas state Can not Move Up

Poor communication from manager. No response or very delayed. Schedule not flexible, work 8 hours a day with no consecutive days off. Lots of rules for pto. Lab responds to CODES. Tech in charge with no pay increase. Call ins constantly with no consequences. Severely short staffed with most people not cross trained. No teamwork ethic. Rude nurses. Constant complaining from coworkers. Run for the hills
none, I can't think of any cons because I enjoyed my time there as a an employee very nice
Cons are stressful work environment. Not family friendly, have to be all about your career Null
Could staff better with ancillary staff, better patient ratios, community hospitals not standardized (each is different) Decent
Unfairness, just another number, hostile work environment No work life balance
In PACU when we have low census we get flexed off having to use PTO or no pay. Which has happened more recently now for 2 years using up to 4 hours PTO weekly to make 36 PACU

evidence.role: Former employee - Licensed Master Social Worker
evidence.text: Unpredictable work environment, overworked, no time for breaks, lack of support from management, obligations to handle several different urgent cases at one time, no work-life balance. Overworked
Work-life balance is not the best. Short-staffed Pharmacist
I do not have any cons for Houston Methodist. Great Company!
